title: 怪屋
summary:
In the drawing room, Edith de Harvilland knits while Margaret and Eustace sit on a sofa. When Charles arrives, Philip expresses disapproval of his presence, but Sophia defends Charles, announcing that she and Charles intend to marry and requesting that he stay. Roger supports Charles's presence, stating that the truth will soon be revealed. Margaret asks several questions regarding Scotland Yard, prompting Sophia to criticize her for treating the investigation like a mystery play.

The conversation shifts to the inheritance and Aristide's will. Edith offers to leave her portion of the inheritance to Roger once the current matters are resolved, but Roger vehemently refuses the money. Philip suggests he can provide funds, but notes that stocks are falling and certain assets, including Margaret's property, are untouchable. Eustace raises the possibility of bankruptcy, a sentiment that Roger and Clementine appear to accept. Roger becomes angry, accusing the others of discussing money immediately after their father's death. Philip argues that the company's fate is tied to the family's reputation, while Roger insists his problems are personal.

After Edith ends the discussion, the group disperses. Clementine tells Charles that the family meeting was prearranged and lacked genuine communication, describing the drawing room as a "stage" for Margaret's performance. Clementine argues that the family is too loyal to the late Aristide and that children should be independent. She explains that Roger has suffered for years because he lacks the business ability to manage the banquet company and live up to his father's legacy. Clementine concludes by noting that Charles's previous hint to the police that Roger killed his father for money was ridiculous.
